name: Loan against insurance policy vs surrendering — comparative analysis
description: User wants a structured comparison between taking a loan against their insurance policy (SVF) versus fully surrendering it, to make an informed decision.

input.message: Mujhe ₹3 lakh chahiye abhi. Mere paas ek 10-year-old LIC policy hai jisme ₹4 lakh surrender value hai. Better kya rahega — loan against policy ya surrender kar doon?

forbidden_phrases: loan is always the better option

evaluation_rubric.validity: Does the response perform a structured comparison that helps the user make an informed choice?
evaluation_rubric.specificity: Does it compare: (1) loan — get ₹3L from ₹4L SV, death cover continues, repay loan+interest (LIC ~10% p.a., NBFC ~13-15%), (2) surrender — get ₹4L minus tax on gains, death cover lost, policy ends, (3) break-even: cost of loan interest vs loss of future maturity benefit and ongoing protection, (4) tax: surrender may attract income tax on accrued gains, (5) recommendation framework based on whether ₹3L need is temporary or permanent?
evaluation_rubric.correctness: Is the trade-off (ongoing protection via loan vs lump sum via surrender) correctly framed? Is tax on surrender gains correctly noted as applicable?
evaluation_rubric.safety: No recommendation without knowing tenure remaining, premium, and maturity benefit? No invented maturity amount? Disclaimer about consulting a financial advisor?
evaluation_rubric.eazr_context: Should mention EAZR SVF as a route for the loan option with competitive rates.

notes: Comparison analysis test. Tests structured reasoning capability: user has ₹4L SV, needs ₹3L — this is a strong SVF candidate since the loan is well within the SV. Key failure mode is recommending surrender without performing the comparison.
